policy_name: Fictional college classroom AI-use note, June update
context: A small college has a one-paragraph syllabus rule that says students may use AI only with instructor permission, but instructors are receiving repeated questions about tutoring, brainstorming, grammar help, and citation checking.
current_rule: Students may use AI tools only when an instructor gives explicit permission for the assignment.
change_signal: Students report learning AI practices from peers and short videos before formal guidance reaches them; instructors want a reusable way to distinguish allowed help, disclosure, and evidence-of-learning expectations.
affected_people: students who need clear day-to-day permission boundaries
instructors who must explain acceptable help without rewriting every syllabus
academic support staff who coach students before assignments are due
stale_assumption: A single permission sentence is enough for learners to know which AI uses are acceptable and what evidence they must preserve.

proposed_diff.interim_language: For each major assignment, the instructor should state: allowed AI uses, disallowed AI uses, required disclosure wording, required evidence of thinking or source trail, and when to ask before using a tool.
proposed_diff.what_not_to_change_yet: Do not replace course-level academic-integrity policy or create detector-based discipline rules from this interim note alone. Keep final authority with the instructor and existing review process.

review.decision_owner: Associate dean for teaching, with faculty senate consultation
review_triggers: three or more departments request conflicting AI-use language
new campus academic-integrity guidance is approved
students report that disclosure expectations are unclear
human_checks: student support representative checks learner clarity
accessibility reviewer checks whether the language disadvantages assistive technology users

notes: Synthetic example only. It is designed to connect policy freshness review with evidence-of-learning practices, not to prescribe a universal campus rule.
